content.problemStatement:
Kulyash loves **perfect squares** and hates **perfect cubes**.

For any natural number NNN,

F(N)=number of perfect squares smaller than or equal to N−number of positive perfect cubes smaller than or equal to NF(N) = \texttt{number of perfect squares smaller than or equal to } N - \texttt{number of positive perfect cubes smaller than or equal to } NF(N)=number of perfect squares smaller than or equal to N−number of positive perfect cubes smaller than or equal to N.

Kulyash gives you an integer XXX and asks you to find the minimum value of NNN, such that F(N)≥XF(N) \geq XF(N)≥X.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input.

The first and only line of each test case contains an integer XXX.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimum value of NNN such that F(N)≥XF(N) \geq XF(N)≥X.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 222 perfect squares from 111 to 444, and 111 perfect cube from 111 to 444, so F(4)=2−1=1F(4) = 2 - 1 = 1F(4)=2−1=1, as required.
Test case 222: There are 555 perfect squares from 111 to 252525, and 222 perfect cubes from 111 to 252525, so F(25)=5−2=3F(25) = 5 - 2 = 3F(25)=5−2=3, as required.
Test case 333: There are 337633763376 perfect squares from 111 to 113973761139737611397376, and 225225225 perfect cubes from 111 to 113973761139737611397376, so F(11397376)=3376−225=3151F(11397376) = 3376 - 225 = 3151F(11397376)=3376−225=3151, as required.
